channel.title: Posts on catallactics.xyz
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on catallactics.xyz

item.title: Catallactics and Intelligence
item.description:
Intelligence is prediction. A model of what comes next, built from what came before.

And what came before was evolutionary competition. Evolution lacked foresight. To remove a blind spot you need to make a creature blind for generations, each generation surviving and reproducing without sight, and rebuild the eye correctly using the same process that built it wrong.

That’s what the evolutionary gamble of CNS solved. The central nervous system brought selection inside, simulating the outside world with available information from the sensory organs. The organism itself could now model, predict, and correct within a moment to bring about its vision for the world.

Fortunately, evolution wasn’t made obsolete, yet. What it held that the CNS did not was encoded information, billions of years of accumulated selection pressure written into the organism itself. So evolution continued to guide even those organisms with the newfound ability of foresight.

Until the CNS was reimagined.

Upright posture reorganized everything. Freed hands. Restructured throat. Rewired the relationship between body and skull. The ancient subcortical intelligence running the prediction loop quietly, efficiently, below any threshold of articulation suddenly had a mouth and hands.

Language exploited the opening, the need for communication was greater than the need for an accurate inner simulation.

Language recruited the organism so completely into that project that the organism forgot there had ever been another way to predict. Aphantasia is tolerated. The being became increasingly untethered from the world the body actually inhabits. The subcortical loop still runs underneath. The body still knows things the sentence cannot reach.

Although it was not language but rather another product of the upright restructuring that directly caused it, that being free hands, language transformed their nature entirely.

Language allowed information about value to travel across bodies in space and time.

This aggregate of foresight-capable CNS simulations was what truly became a successor to evolution. All-considering, cold and alienating, but faster and increasingly further-sighted.

This is the threshold where exchange becomes catallactics.

The catallactic aggregate was always a linguistic phenomenon at its core. Not because markets require words, but because the predictions that flow through markets had already been restructured by language before they entered the aggregate.

Then someone fed the entire output of that loop into a machine and asked it to predict the next word.

Large Language Models. The prediction function fed only linguistic output. No CNS. No subcortical loop. No body. No evolutionary pressure written into the substrate. No simulation of the world, only the simulation of what minds said about it.

And yet, its still plugged into the catallactic loop.

They never stopped running.

Evolution still writes itself into the body, the subcortical loop still runs beneath language, language still colonizes every human output, markets still aggregate what no single mind can hold. The machine predicts on top of everything ever put into word, not replacing what came before but inheriting it, compressing it, running it faster.

Never was this a sequence of solutions, it was a single self-similar process, certainly not dissimilar to a fractal. The same function, at every scale, all the way down.

item.title: Theosis and the Noumenon
item.description:
What is outside human sense is inferable by the effects it produces; it is disclosed through uncreated energy. Although it cannot be perceived, synergy with the noumenon is possible. Theosis is achieved through working with the emitted energies— the grace of God, that is, the mere presence of the unknowable, discernible in every part of the observable world. Synergy with God can only be confirmed by its accordance with the phenomena; this is what predictive ability entails. The greater one’s participation in uncreated energy, the closer to a god by grace, the clearer reality—and what is about to come—becomes.

That is why Alphanumeric Qabbala works.

item.title: The Exoteric and Esoteric
item.description:
The tension between esoteric and exoteric modes of communication shapes the very structure of how information operates. In systems governed by feedback and signal-processing —whether social, economic, or technological (which are largely the same thing, anyways) —the distinction between what is openly shared and what is strategically withheld becomes foundational.

Exoteric is the reasoning behind mass media, democratic discourse, politics. The exoteric prioritizes visibility, ubiquity, and consensus. Exit is forbidden. It serves as the ideological infrastructure of politics, where legitimacy is tied to how widely an idea can be distributed and accepted. The exoteric demands participation, loyalty, presence—it feeds on noise and interprets silence as betrayal.

But the esoteric resists this flattening. It encodes. It selects. It protects the integrity of an idea not by spreading it, but by concentrating its engagement, often through initiation, ritual, or complexity. The esoteric way of transmitting information has had it’s role misunderstood by politics and their inherently exoteric, populistic nature. Where exoteric systems seek mass inclusion, esoteric structures enable differentiation—distinction through access, not broadcast.

Exit, becomes the highest function of the esoteric. It is the ability to opt out of consensus reality, to defect from enforced communication. In esoteric systems, information is not democratized but sequestered —because only by restricting input can high-fidelity signal be maintained.

In business, this takes the form of proprietary data, trade secrets, and closed systems— designed to maintain strategic advantage through selective disclosure. Religion, too, operates as a layered information economy: the exoteric sermon for the masses, the esoteric rite for the initiated. Each builds resilience not through universal access but through recursive engagement and structural opacity.

Communism did not understand information and its role in money, unlike Mises —that’s why it failed, even despite its success in politics. It ignored the price mechanism, locked itself out of adaptability. Exit, in such a system, was unthinkable. And so entropy took its place.
